facts.statement: Intuitive is committed to advancing minimally invasive care through a comprehensive ecosystem of products and services including systems, instruments and accessories, learning, and services connected by a digital portfolio that enables actionable digital insights across the care continuum.
Total revenue for FY2025 was $7.124 billion, as reported under the concept RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax.
Net income for FY2025 was $1.798 billion.
Operating cash flow for FY2025 was $1.814 billion.
Research and development expense for FY2025 was $998.8 million.
As of FY2025, stockholders' equity was $16.43 billion and cash and equivalents were $2.03 billion.
Intuitive Surgical is classified under SIC code description 'Orthopedic, Prosthetic & Surgical Appliances & Supplies' and trades on Nasdaq under the ticker ISRG.
The Company manages its business activities on a consolidated basis and operates in one reportable segment, with the CEO serving as the Chief Operating Decision Maker.
Domestic revenue accounted for 68% of total revenue in FY2025, while revenue from outside the U.S. markets accounted for 32%.
The 10-K references revenue categories including Systems, Instruments and Accessories, and Service, as well as variable lease revenue from usage-based arrangements.
The Company has a common stock repurchase program, with repurchase activity reported for FY2023, FY2024, and FY2025.
Share-based compensation expense before income taxes was $802.8 million in FY2025, up from $688.1 million in FY2024 and $598.2 million in FY2023.
The Company's effective tax rate for FY2025 was 13.1%, with total income tax expense of $434.8 million on pre-tax income of $3.31 billion.
Diluted net income per share for FY2025 was $7.87, compared to $6.42 in FY2024 and $5.03 in FY2023.
David J. Rosa serves as Chief Executive Officer and Director, signing the 10-K on February 3, 2026.
Jamie E. Samath serves as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer (Principal Financial Officer).
Gary S. Guthart, Ph.D. serves as Executive Chair of the Board of Directors, having transitioned from CEO to Executive Chair as part of a leadership succession.
The Board nominees include 10 directors, 8 of whom are independent, with Craig H. Barratt, Ph.D. serving as Lead Independent Director.

The Company has a single class of stock with equal voting rights, majority voting standard for uncontested director elections, a director resignation policy, proxy access for stockholders, and stockholder right to call a special meeting.
The aggregate market value of voting and non-voting common equity held by non-affiliates as of June 30, 2025 was approximately $193.9 billion.
As of January 28, 2026, there were 355,130,237 shares of common stock outstanding.

Forward-looking risk factors include macroeconomic impacts on customer spending and costs (including tariffs, inflation, and interest rates), supply chain disruptions, curtailed hospital capital spending, delays in FDA and other regulatory approvals, healthcare reform legislation, intellectual property litigation, and risks associated with international operations including the joint venture with Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical.

On November 17, 2025, Brian E. Miller, Ph.D., the Company's Head of Digital and AI Strategy, adopted a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan providing for the potential sale of up to 35,344 shares of common stock until December 15, 2026.

During 2025, the Company met with stockholders representing approximately 45% of outstanding shares, discussing business strategy, financial performance, corporate governance, executive compensation, and sustainability.
